title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn and Jules Reed discuss the potential motives regarding Jackie Avery and letters that may have caused Helen to pretend she had gone abroad. Mrs. Cochran informs them that Mr. Fenn visited earlier that afternoon, claiming Gwendolyn was expecting him, as was a second man in a large yellow car. Jules calls Fenn, who claims he received an urgent call from the office regarding a large sum of money, but denies being the caller. Jules then calls Avery, who reveals that a man impersonating Jules called him to request an urgent meeting regarding money. Gwendolyn suggests that either Fenn or Jack Afrik is attempting to create a false alibi or frame the other. Jules suspects Fenn, but Miss Marple suggests another possibility.

Jules calls Colonel Erskine in Northumberland to verify his whereabouts. Gwendolyn uses a pretext about the Linscott Blake Mansion to engage him in conversation. Erskine reveals he is at home performing housework because his wife is visiting her sister and the maid is away. Gwendolyn concludes that Erskine is ruled out as a suspect for Lily Greenbank's murder, though Miss Marple remains grave and worried.

Later, Gwendolyn reflects on the chaotic morning. Detective Inspector Primer and Inspector Premerle arrive to investigate the murder of Lily Kimber. Jules directs the inspectors to the terrace and the nursery. Jules then encourages Gwendolyn to share her private observations with Premerle. Gwendolyn describes her inexplicable sense of familiarity with the house, claiming she remembers the wallpaper and layout from her own childhood.
